Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, indicating significant renal impairment. There is also elevated ALT, suggesting possible hepatic involvement. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for acute or chronic kidney disease, possibly with metabolic derangements (e.g., diabetes, ketoacidosis). Immediate clinical evaluation and nephrology referral are recommended.
